advantages: Increases effective training diversity without new labeling cost — especially valuable when annotations are expensive.
Encourages task-appropriate invariances (pose, lighting, wording) that match real-world variation at inference.
Often pairs cleanly with existing training loops via on-the-fly transforms — no need to store every synthetic copy.
Complements weight regularization and early stopping; together they address overfitting from multiple angles.
disadvantages: Wrong transforms violate labels and can hurt accuracy more than no augmentation — domain knowledge is required.
Heavy augmentation increases training time per epoch and can slow convergence if perturbations are too strong.
Text and tabular augmentation is harder to validate than image flips — paraphrase or SMOTE can introduce label noise.
Does not replace collecting real diverse data when deployment distribution differs systematically from any transform.
